top_signals.title: EasyJet’s summer sale reaches Wall Street
top_signals.summary: Europe’s famed budget airline has become a potential takeover target as war in Iran hits industry
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: FirstFT: US in talks to widen nuclear arms deployments in Europe
top_signals.summary: Also in today’s newsletter: Alphabet to raise $80bn from share sale and gold overtakes US Treasuries as the world’s leading reserve asset
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Intel CEO praises 'friend' Nvidia and TSMC despite rising competition
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Biotech takeover target Abivax sinks over 30% as cancer cases cloud trial data for bowel disease drug
top_signals.summary: A clinical trial update wiped out billions in the French company's market cap and raised questions over a potential takeover.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Castlelake: the private credit lender taking a run at easyJet
top_signals.summary: The $37bn investment manager is little known in the UK, but that could change if it bids on the British budget carrier

top_signals.title: Vitalik Buterin proposes options-based synthetic assets to avoid liquidations and reduce reliance on real-time oracles
top_signals.summary: Buterin proposed options-based synthetic assets that avoid liquidation risk and rely on slow oracles instead of real-time price feeds.
